explicit_text: I was born in Xiengkhouang Province, Laos, in 1954. It is where my early life began before the turmoil of war changed everything. Growing up there, I experienced the impact of the Secret War, which deeply affected my family and community. Xiengkhouang holds my roots and memories before I had to leave for safety.
implicit_text: I was born in a mountainous region of northeastern Laos in 1954. It is where my early life began before the turmoil of war changed everything. Growing up there, I experienced the impact of the Secret War, which deeply affected my family and community. This province holds my roots and memories before I had to leave for safety.

explicit_text: Laos is my homeland where I was born and raised until the mid-1980s. My family and I lived there until we had to flee due to the conflicts related to the Secret War. Crossing the Mekong River in 1984 marked our departure from Laos, a place filled with both my childhood memories and the pain of losing my father. Laos will always be an important part of who I am.
implicit_text: My homeland is where I was born and raised until the mid-1980s. My family and I lived there until we had to flee due to the conflicts related to the Secret War. Crossing a major Southeast Asian river in 1984 marked our departure from that country, a place filled with both my childhood memories and the pain of losing my father. This nation will always be an important part of who I am.

explicit_text: The Mekong River was a significant and challenging part of my journey when my family and I fled Laos in 1984. Crossing that river was a dangerous and emotional moment as we left behind our home and everything familiar. It symbolized the start of a difficult path toward safety and a new life. The river is forever etched in my memory as a boundary between my past and future.
implicit_text: A great river in Southeast Asia was a significant and challenging part of my journey when my family and I fled our homeland in 1984. Crossing that river was a dangerous and emotional moment as we left behind our home and everything familiar. It symbolized the start of a difficult path toward safety and a new life. The river is forever etched in my memory as a boundary between my past and future.

explicit_text: After crossing the Mekong River, my family and I lived in Thailand from 1984 until 1987. Thailand was a temporary refuge where we stayed before immigrating to the United States. Those years were filled with uncertainty but also hope for a better future. Thailand was a crucial stop in our journey to safety and stability.
implicit_text: After crossing the great river, my family and I lived in a neighboring country to the west from 1984 until 1987. This nation was a temporary refuge where we stayed before immigrating to the United States. Those years were filled with uncertainty but also hope for a better future. This country was a crucial stop in our journey to safety and stability.

explicit_text: In 1987, my family and I arrived in the United States to start a new life. I was young and newly married when we came, and we settled in Wisconsin after first landing in Seattle. The U.S. gave me a chance to rebuild after the hardships of war and displacement. It has become my home where I continue to preserve my culture and memories.
implicit_text: In 1987, my family and I arrived in North America to start a new life. I was young and newly married when we came, and we settled in a Midwestern state after first landing in a city on the West Coast. This country gave me a chance to rebuild after the hardships of war and displacement. It has become my home where I continue to preserve my culture and memories.

explicit_text: Seattle was the first place I landed when I arrived in the United States in 1987. It was a brief stop before I took a connecting flight to Wisconsin, where I would eventually settle. Seattle represents the gateway to my new life in America after fleeing Laos and Thailand. It was the first step in a long journey toward stability.
implicit_text: This West Coast city was the first place I landed when I arrived in North America in 1987. It was a brief stop before I took a connecting flight to a Midwestern state, where I would eventually settle. The city represents the gateway to my new life in America after fleeing my homeland and its neighboring refuge. It was the first step in a long journey toward stability.

explicit_text: Wisconsin became my home after arriving in the United States in 1987. After landing in Seattle, I took a connecting flight to Wisconsin, where I settled with my family. It is where I have lived since and where I continue to play the flute and keep my Hmong culture alive. Wisconsin is where I rebuilt my life after so much loss.

It is where I have lived since and where I continue to play the flute and keep my Hmong culture alive. This state is where I rebuilt my life after so much loss.

explicit_text: The Secret War deeply affected my family and my life. My father fought against the communist Vietnamese during this conflict and was killed at a base camp. His death left me orphaned and devastated, which led me to learn to play the flute as a way to express my sorrow. The Secret War is a painful chapter that shaped my childhood and my connection to my culture.

His death left me orphaned and devastated, which led me to learn to play the flute as a way to express my sorrow. This hidden war is a painful chapter that shaped my childhood and my connection to my culture.

explicit_text: The qeej is an important musical instrument in Hmong culture, alongside the flute that I play. I hope that the younger generation learns to play the qeej as well, to keep our traditions alive. It represents our cultural identity and is a vital part of our music and storytelling. I am willing to teach anyone interested in learning these instruments to preserve our heritage.
